hitchens_question: When we read that AI companies have a "responsibility" to safeguard their models from exploitation while simultaneously noting that Dutch factories are cranking out combat robots for Ukraine and the Pentagon rushes to deploy drone swarms, are we witnessing the birth of a new moral language designed specifically to obscure the fact that we are enthusiastically automating slaughter? And if so, at what precise point did we decide that the appropriate response to human wickedness was to remove humans from the equation entirely, leaving our killing to the same algorithms that can't reliably recommend a decent restaurant?
